H1104.sd: to make away with (specifically by swallowing); generally, to destroy
H1104.kd: cover, destroy, devour, eat up, be at end, spend up, swallow down (up).

H1105.kd: devouring, that which he hath swallowed up.

H1106.sd: Bela, the name of a place, also of an Edomite and of two Israelites

H1107.dv: or בַּלְעֲדֵי; constructive plural from H1077 (בַּל) and H5703 (עַד), not till, i.e. (as preposition or adverb)

H1108.sd: a Belaite (collectively) or descendants of Bela

H1109.sd: Bilam, a Mesopotamian prophet; also a place in Palestine
H1109.dv: probably from H1077 (בַּל) and H5971 (עַם); not (of the) people, i.e. foreigner;

H1112.dv: or בֵּלְאשַׁצַּרlemma בֵּלְאשַׁצּר missing vowel, corrected to בֵּלְאשַׁצַּר; of foreign origin (compare H1095 (בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר));

H1115.sd: properly, a failure of, i.e. (used only as a negative particle, usually with a prepositional prefix) not, except, without, unless, besides, because not, until, etc.
H1115.kd: because un(satiable), beside, but, [phrase] continual, except, from, lest, neither, no more, none, not, nothing, save, that no, without.
H1115.dv: constructive feminine of H1086 (בָּלָה) (equivalent to H1097 (בְּלִי));

H1116.dv: from an unused root (meaning to be high);

H1118.dv: probably from H4107 (מָהַל) with prepositional prefix; with pruning;

H1120.sd: Bamoth or Bamoth-Baal, a place East of the Jordan
H1120.dv: plural of H1116 (בָּמָה); heights; or (fully) בָּמוֹת בַּעַל; from the same and H1168 (בַּעַל); heights of Baal;

H1121.sd: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
H1121.kd: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch, breed, [phrase] (young) bullock, [phrase] (young) calf, [idiom] came up in, child, colt, [idiom] common, [idiom] corn, daughter, [idiom] of first, [phrase] firstborn, foal, [phrase] very fruitful, [phrase] postage, [idiom] in, [phrase] kid, [phrase] lamb, ([phrase]) man, meet, [phrase] mighty, [phrase] nephew, old, ([phrase]) people, [phrase] rebel, [phrase] robber, [idiom] servant born, [idiom] soldier, son, [phrase] spark, [phrase] steward, [phrase] stranger, [idiom] surely, them of, [phrase] tumultuous one, [phrase] valiant(-est), whelp, worthy, young (one), youth.

H1123.sd: {a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense}

H1125.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H40 (אֲבִימֶלֶךְ); (the) son of Abinadab;

H1126.sd: Ben-Oni, the original name of Benjamin

H1127.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H1397 (גֶּבֶר); son of (the) hero;

H1128.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and a derivative of H1856 (דָּקַר); son of piercing (or of a lance);

H1129.kd: (begin to) build(-er), obtain children, make, repair, set (up), [idiom] surely.

H1130.sd: Ben-Hadad, the name of several Syrian kings
H1130.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H1908 (הֲדַד); son of Hadad;

H1132.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2105 (זוֹחֵת); son of Zocheth;

H1133.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2354 (חוּר); son of Chur;

H1134.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2428 (חַיִל); son of might;

H1135.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2605 (חָנָן); son of Chanan;

H1136.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2617 (חֵסֵד); son of kindness;

H1137.sd: Bani, the name of five Israelites

H1139.sd: sons of lightning, Bene-berak, a place in Palestine
H1139.dv: from the plural construction of H1121 (בֵּן) and H1300 (בָּרָק);

H1141.sd: Benajah, the name of twelve Israelites

H1142.sd: Bene-Jaakan, a place in the Desert

H1143.sd: a double interval, i.e. the space between two armies

H1144.sd: Binjamin, youngest son of Jacob; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory
H1144.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H3225 (יָמִין); son of (the) right hand;

H1145.sd: a Benjaminite, or descendent of Benjamin

H1148.dv: probably from H1121 (בֵּן) with pron. suff.; our son;

H1151.dv: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H5971 (עַם) with pronomial suffix; son of my people;

H1152.dv: from H5475 (סוֹד) and H3050 (יָהּ) with prepositional prefix; in (the) counsel of Jehovah;

H1154.dv: from an unused root meaning to be sour;

H1156.kd: ask, desire, make (petition), pray, request, seek.

H1157.sd: in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
H1157.kd: about, at by (means of), for, over, through, up (-on), within.

H1158.sd: to gush over, i.e. to swell; (figuratively) to desire earnestly; by implication to ask
H1158.kd: cause, inquire, seek up, swell out.

H1160.sd: Beor, the name of the father of an Edomitish king; also of that of Balaam
H1160.dv: from H1197 (בָּעַר) (in the sense of burning); a lamp;

H1162.sd: Boaz, the ancestor of David; also the name of a pillar in front of the temple
H1162.dv: from an unused root of uncertain meaning;

H1165.dv: from H1197 (בָּעַר) (in the sense of eating);

H1166.sd: to be master; hence, to marry
H1166.kd: have dominion (over), be husband, marry(-ried, [idiom] wife).
H1166.dv: a primitive root; also as denominative from H1167 (בַּעַל)

H1167.sd: a master; hence, a husband, or (figuratively) owner (often used with another noun in modifications of this latter sense)
H1167.kd: [phrase] archer, [phrase] babbler, [phrase] bird, captain, chief man, [phrase] confederate, [phrase] have to do, [phrase] dreamer, those to whom it is due, [phrase] furious, those that are given to it, great, [phrase] hairy, he that hath it, have, [phrase] horseman, husband, lord, man, [phrase] married, master, person, [phrase] sworn, they of.

H1170.sd: Baal-Berith, a special deity of the Shechemites
H1170.dv: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H1285 (בְּרִית); Baal of (the) covenant;

H1171.dv: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H1409 (גָּד); Baal of Fortune;

H1173.sd: Baalah, the name of three places in Palestine

H1175.kd: Bealoth, in Aloth (by mistake for a plural from H5927 (עָלָה) with prepositional prefix).

H1176.dv: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H2070 (זְבוּב); Baal of (the) Fly;

H1177.dv: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H2603 (חָנַן); possessor of grace;

H1179.dv: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H2768 (חֶרְמוֹן); possessor of Hermon;

H1180.sd: Baali, a symbolical name for Jehovah
H1180.dv: from H1167 (בַּעַל) with pron. suff.; my master;

H1181.sd: Baale-Bamoth, a place East of the Jordan

H1182.dv: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H3045 (יָדַע); Baal has known;

H1183.dv: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah (is) master;

H1184.dv: from the plural of H1167 (בַּעַל) and H3063 (יְהוּדָה); masters of Judah;

H1185.dv: probably from a derivative of H5965 (עָלַס) with prepositional prefix; in exultation;

H1186.sd: Baal-Meon, a place East of the Jordan

H1188.dv: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and the plural of H6556 (פֶּרֶץ); possessor of breaches;

H1190.sd: Baal of Shalishah, Baal-Shalishah, a place in Palestine

H1192.dv: from H1172 (בַּעֲלָה) and H875 (בְּאֵר); mistress of a well;

H1193.dv: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H8558 (תָּמָר); possessor of (the) palm-tree;

H1194.sd: Beon, a place East of the Jordan
H1194.dv: probably a contraction of H1010 (בֵּית בַּעַל מְעוֹן);

H1195.sd: Banana, the name of four Israelite

H1196.sd: Baanah, the name of four Israelites
H1196.dv: from a derivative of H6031 (עָנָה) with prepositional prefix; in affliction

H1197.sd: to kindle, i.e. consume (by fire or by eating); to be(-come) brutish

H1201.dv: from an unused root meaning to stink; offensiveness;

H1202.dv: from H6213 (עָשָׂה) and H3050 (יָהּ) with a prepositional prefix; in (the) work of Jah;

H1203.sd: Beeshterah, a place East of the Jordan
H1203.dv: from H6251 (עַשְׁתְּרָה) (as singular of H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת)) with a prepositional prefix; with Ashtoreth;

H1209.sd: Betsai, the name of two Israelites

H1211.dv: from an unused root apparently meaning to peel;

H1212.sd: Betsalel, the name of two Israelites
H1212.dv: probably from H6738 (צֵל) and H410 (אֵל) with a prepositional prefix; in (the) shadow (i.e. protection) of God;

H1216.sd: perhaps to swell up, i.e. blister

H1218.dv: from H1216 (בָּצֵק); a swell of ground;

H1221.sd: Betser, a place in Palestine; also an Israelite

H1227.dv: the same as H1228 (בַּקְבֻּק)xlit baqbûk corrected to baqbuq;

H1228.sd: a bottle (from the gurgling in emptying)

H1234.sd: to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open
H1234.kd: make a breach, break forth (into, out, in pieces, through, up), be ready to burst, cleave (asunder), cut out, divide, hatch, rend (asunder), rip up, tear, win.

H1237.sd: properly, a split, i.e. a wide level valley between mountains

H1239.sd: properly, to plough, or (generally) break forth, i.e. (figuratively) to inspect, admire, care for, consider

H1240.sd: {properly, to plough, or (generally) break forth, i.e. (figuratively) to inspect, admire, care for, consider}

H1241.sd: beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gender (as used for plowing); collectively, a herd
H1241.kd: beeve, bull ([phrase] -ock), [phrase] calf, [phrase] cow, great (cattle), [phrase] heifer, herd, kine, ox.

H1245.kd: ask, beg, beseech, desire, enquire, get, make inquisition, procure, (make) request, require, seek (for).

H1253.sd: vegetable lye (from its cleansing); used as a soap forwashing, or a flux formetals

H1254.sd: (absolutely) to create; (qualified) to cut down (a wood), select, feed (as formative processes)
H1254.kd: choose, create (creator), cut down, dispatch, do, make (fat).

H1256.dv: from H1254 (בָּרָא) and H8050 (שְׁמוּאֵל); Jah has created;

H1260.sd: Bered, the name of a place south of Palestine, also of an Israelite

H1262.sd: to select; to feed; to render clear
H1262.dv: a primitive root; to select; also (as denominative from H1250 (בָּר)) to feed; also (as equivalent to H1305 (בָּרַר)) to render clear (Ecclesiastes 3:18)

H1263.sd: Baruk, the name of three Israelites

H1265.sd: a cypress (?) tree; hence, a lance or a musical instrument (as made of that wood)

H1268.sd: Berothah or Berothai, a place north of Palestine
H1268.dv: or בֵּרֹתַי; probably from H1266 (בְּרוֹת)lemma בֻּרוֹת first vowel, corrected to בְּרוֹת; cypress or cypresslike;

H1269.dv: probably feminine plural from an unused root (apparently mean to pierce); holes;

H1270.sd: iron (as cutting); by extension, an iron implement
H1270.dv: perhaps from the root of H1269 (בִּרְזוֹת);

H1271.sd: Barzillai, the name of three Israelites

H1272.sd: to bolt, i.e. figuratively, to flee suddenly
H1272.kd: chase (away); drive away, fain, flee (away), put to flight, make haste, reach, run away, shoot.

H1273.sd: a Barchumite, or native of Bachurim

H1276.sd: (only in the plural and with the article) the Berites, a place in Palestine

H1277.dv: from H1254 (בָּרָא) (in the sense of H1262 (בָּרָה));

H1281.sd: a fugitive, i.e. the serpent (as fleeing), and the constellation by that name

H1283.sd: Beriah, the name of four Israelites
H1283.dv: apparently from the feminine of H7451 (רַע) with a prepositional prefix; in trouble;

H1284.sd: a Beriite (collectively) or descendants of Beriah

H1285.sd: a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh)

H1288.sd: to kneel; by implication to bless God (as an act of adoration), and (vice-versa) man (as a benefit); also (by euphemism) to curse (God or the king, as treason)
H1288.kd: [idiom] abundantly, [idiom] altogether, [idiom] at all, blaspheme, bless, congratulate, curse, [idiom] greatly, [idiom] indeed, kneel (down), praise, salute, [idiom] still, thank.

H1289.sd: {to kneel; by implication to bless God (as an act of adoration), and (vice-versa) man (as a benefit); also (by euphemism) to curse (God or the king, as treason)}

H1292.sd: Barakel, the father of one of Job's friends
H1292.dv: from H1288 (בָרַךְ) and H410 (אֵל), God has blessed;

H1294.sd: Berakah, the name of an Israelite, and also of a valley in Palestine

H1295.sd: a reservoir (at which camels kneel as a resting-place)

H1296.sd: Berekjah, the name of six Israelites
H1296.dv: or בֶּרֶכְיָהוּ; from H1290 (בֶּרֶךְ) and H3050 (יָהּ); knee (i.e. blessing) of Jah;

H1297.sd: properly, highly, i.e. surely; but used adversatively, however
H1297.dv: (Aramaic) perhaps from H7313 (רוּם) with a prepositional prefix;

H1300.sd: lightning; by analogy, a gleam; concretely, a flashing sword

H1304.sd: a gem (as flashing), perhaps the emerald
H1304.dv: or בָּרְקַתxlit bârᵉkath corrected to bârᵉqath; from H1300 (בָּרָק);

H1305.kd: make bright, choice, chosen, cleanse (be clean), clearly, polished, (shew self) pure(-ify), purge (out).

H1306.dv: probably from H7562 (רֶשַׁע) with a prepositional prefix; with wickedness;

H1307.sd: a Berothite, or inhabitant of Berothai

H1309.sd: glad tidings; by implication, reward forgood news

H1310.sd: properly, to boil up; hence, to be done in cooking; figuratively to ripen
H1310.kd: bake, boil, bring forth, roast, seethe, sod (be sodden).

H1313.dv: from an unused root meaning to be fragrant; (compare H5561 (סַם))

H1314.sd: fragrance; by implication, spicery; also the balsam plant
H1314.dv: or בֹּשֶׂם; from the same as H1313 (בָּשָׂם);

H1315.sd: Bosmath, the name of a wife of Esau, and of a daughter of Solomon
H1315.dv: feminine of H1314 (בֶּשֶׂם) (the second form); fragrance;

H1316.sd: Bashan (often with the article), a region East of the Jordan

H1319.sd: properly, to be fresh, i.e. full (rosy, (figuratively) cheerful); to announce (glad news)
H1319.kd: messenger, preach, publish, shew forth, (bear, bring, carry, preach, good, tell good) tidings.

H1320.sd: flesh (from its freshness); by extension, body, person; also (by euphemistically) the pudenda of aman
H1320.kd: body, (fat, lean) flesh(-ed), kin, (man-) kind, [phrase] nakedness, self, skin.

H1321.sd: {flesh (from its freshness); by extension, body, person; also (by euphemistically) the pudenda of aman}

H1322.sd: shame (the feeling and the condition, as well as its cause); by implication (specifically) an idol
H1322.kd: ashamed, confusion, [phrase] greatly, (put to) shame(-ful thing).

H1323.sd: a daughter (used in the same wide sense as other terms of relationship, literally and figuratively)
H1323.kd: apple (of the eye), branch, company, daughter, [idiom] first, [idiom] old, [phrase] owl, town, village.

H1324.sd: a bath or Hebrew measure (as a means of division) of liquids
H1324.dv: probably from the same as H1327 (בַּתָּה);

H1325.sd: {a bath or Hebrew measure (as a means of division) of liquids}

H1326.dv: probably an orthographical variation for H1327 (בַּתָּה);

H1327.dv: feminine from an unused root (meaning to break in pieces);

H1328.sd: Bethuel, the name of a nephew of Abraham, and of a place in Palestine
H1328.dv: apparently from the same as H1326 (בָּתָה) and H410 (אֵל); destroyed of God;

H1329.sd: Bethul (i.e. Bethuel), a place in Palestine

H1330.sd: a virgin (from her privacy); sometimes (by continuation) a bride; also (figuratively) a city or state
H1330.dv: feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to separate;

H1331.sd: (collectively and abstractly) virginity; by implication and concretely, the tokens of it
H1331.dv: masculine plural of the same as H1330 (בְּתוּלָה);

H1332.dv: from H1323 (בַּת) and H3050 (יָהּ); daughter (i.e. worshipper) of Jah;

H1337.dv: from H1323 (בַּת) and a masculine plural from H7227 (רַב);

H1338.sd: Bithron, a place East of the Jordan
H1338.dv: from H1334 (בָּתַר); (with the article) the craggy spot;

H1339.dv: from H1323 (בַּת) and H7651 (שֶׁבַע) (in the sense of H7650 (שָׁבַע)); daughter of an oath;

H1340.dv: from H1323 (בַּת) and H7771 (שׁוֹעַ); daughter of wealth; the same as H1339 (בַּת־שֶׁבַע)

H1342.sd: to mount up; hence, in general, to rise, (figuratively) be majestic
H1342.kd: gloriously, grow up, increase, be risen, triumph.

H1345.dv: from H1342 (גָּאָה) and H410 (אֵל); majesty of God;
